title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

seo.title: How to Make Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani — Easy Indian Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani. This easy Indian recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
longTailKeywords: how to make Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani
easy Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani recipe
authentic Indian Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani
lowCompetitionKeywords: homemade Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani
Indian Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani Recipe
h2Headings: What is Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Indian Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani — a beloved Indian classic

description: Indulge in the rich and creamy flavors of classic Butter Chicken, a beloved Indian delicacy known for its tender chicken pieces simmered in a luscious tomato-based gravy. This Murgh Makhani recipe offers a perfect balance of sweet, tangy, and mildly spicy notes, making it a comforting and satisfying meal. Prepare to be enchanted by its velvety texture and aromatic spices, a true taste of traditional Indian hospitality.

ingredients.item: Boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, red chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, salt, and lemon juice. Mix well to coat the chicken evenly. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours, or even overnight for best results.

instructions.title: Cook the Marinated Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of butter in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Cook until lightly browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per side. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add the remaining 3 tablespoons of butter. Once melted, add green cardamoms and cinnamon stick and saute for 30 seconds. Add the finely chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in another tablespoon of ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Tomato Gravy
instructions.text: Add the tomato puree or crushed tomatoes to the pan. Season with 1 teaspoon red chili powder, 1/2 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon sugar, and salt to taste. Add the soaked cashews (drained). Bring to a gentle simmer and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens and the oil starts to separate from the sides.

instructions.title: Blend to a Smooth Consistency
instructions.text: Remove the whole spices (cardamom and cinnamon stick) if desired. Carefully transfer the tomato mixture to a blender or use an immersion blender. Blend until very smooth and creamy. For an extra silky texture, you can strain the gravy through a fine-mesh sieve, pressing down on the solids.

instructions.title: Combine and Finish
instructions.text: Return the smooth gravy to the pan over low heat. Add the cooked chicken pieces and stir to coat. Pour in the heavy cream and crushed kasoori methi. Simmer for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to absorb the sauce. Adjust seasoning if necessary.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Garnish with a drizzle of fresh cream and freshly chopped coriander leaves. Serve hot with naan bread, roti, basmati rice, or jeera rice. Enjoy your homemade Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani!

tips: For extra tender and flavorful chicken, marinate the chicken overnight. This allows the spices to deeply penetrate the meat.
Do not skip the step of blending the gravy until very smooth. This is crucial for achieving the signature silky, luxurious texture of authentic Butter Chicken.
Always finish your Butter Chicken with a drizzle of fresh cream and a sprinkle of crushed Kasoori Methi (dried fenugreek leaves) and fresh coriander for an aromatic and visually appealing dish.
article: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani is a beloved Indian Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across Indian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
